food_subgroup: Steamed or baked coconut-based layered cake

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry exists for Tapak Kuda or Coco Dinosaur Tapak Kuda. Closest parent 'coconut cake food product' (FOODON_03315091) used as the nearest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to Tapak Kuda exists. FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Cakes and pastries) used as the closest parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps to Tapak Kuda or Coco Dinosaur Tapak Kuda. SNOMED CT anchor omitted.

serving.natural_language_portion: One piece of Coco Dinosaur Tapak Kuda as served at Kazo Premium weighs approximately 200 g, reflecting a standard single-portion layered coconut cake slice sold at SGD 18.80.

nutrient_source.title: Kuih Lapis (Layered Coconut Cake) — HPB Singapore Foodo / USDA FoodData Central coconut-based steamed cake composite
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct HPB or USDA entry exists for 'Coco Dinosaur Tapak Kuda' as a branded product. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo data for Kuih Lapis (steamed layered coconut cake) and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central entries for coconut milk-based steamed cakes. Tapak Kuda is a traditional Singaporean horseshoe-shaped steamed coconut and water chestnut flour cake; its macronutrient profile is closely analogous to Kuih Lapis. Values represent a best-estimate composite for the standard recipe. Branded premium variants (Kazo Premium) may differ slightly.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of rice flour and tapioca starch-based steamed cakes — analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for rice-flour-based Asian cakes and kuih
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Tapak Kuda or Coco Dinosaur Tapak Kuda. GI of 65 is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database entries for rice flour-based steamed Asian cakes and kuih-type confections, which cluster in the GI 60–70 range. The high sugar and refined starch content (rice flour, tapioca starch, sugar) supports a medium GI estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 68.4) / 100 ≈ 44.5 for a 200 g serving (34.2 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 2).

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~55 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Coco Dinosaur Tapak Kuda is low in sodium (~110 mg per serving), making it acceptable for hypertensive individuals when consumed as an occasional treat, though the high sugar content warrants moderation to avoid weight-related blood pressure elevation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~44.5 per serving) driven by refined rice flour, tapioca starch, and added sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid Coco Dinosaur Tapak Kuda due to its high glycemic load of approximately 44.5 per serving, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; if consumed, portion size should be strictly limited to half a piece or less.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat from coconut milk (~7.4 g per 100 g) and elevated added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high saturated fat content from coconut milk (~14.8 g per serving) and significant added sugar in Coco Dinosaur Tapak Kuda may adversely affect lipid profiles; individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional indulgence and limit serving size.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coco Dinosaur Tapak Kuda is a premium Singaporean steamed layered coconut cake sold as a single piece at approximately SGD 18.80 by Kazo Premium at Funan Mall. 'Tapak Kuda' means 'horseshoe' in Malay, referring to the traditional horseshoe-shaped mould used to form the cake. The 'Coco Dinosaur' branding refers to the use of Milo Dinosaur-style Ovaltine or cocoa powder dusting and a coconut-forward flavour profile, representing a modern fusion of traditional Singaporean kuih aesthetics with contemporary café-style presentation. The cake is made from layers of coconut milk, rice flour, tapioca starch, sugar, and pandan extract, steamed in alternating coloured layers to create a visually striking cross-section. The texture is soft, slightly chewy, and gelatinous, with a rich coconut aroma and a sweet, mildly pandan-scented flavour. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at approximately 218 kcal per 100 g, with a high carbohydrate content (34.2 g per 100 g) from refined starches and added sugar, and a moderate fat content (8.5 g per 100 g) predominantly from saturated coconut fat. The glycemic load per serving is high (~44.5), making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. It is naturally gluten-free, dairy-free, vegan, and halal. Clinically, the primary concerns are the high glycemic load and saturated fat from coconut milk, which are relevant for diabetes and cardiovascular disease management.

confidence_reason: No direct HPB, ICMR-NIN, or USDA entry exists for Coco Dinosaur Tapak Kuda as a branded product. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo data for Kuih Lapis and analogous coconut-based steamed cakes, which share the same primary ingredients and preparation method. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database entries for rice flour-based Asian steamed cakes. Confidence is medium because the branded premium variant may differ from the standard recipe in sugar content, portion size, and ingredient ratios.
